data.title: WLFI token holders may be locked indefinitely under World Liberty's proposal. Justin Sun criticizes plan, early investors face 4-year lockups.
data.description: World Liberty Financial's latest vesting plan has sparked controversy, with Justin Sun labeling it an "absurd governance scam." The plan entails early investors being subjected to 4-year token lockups or potentially indefinite freezes. This move has raised concerns within the community due to its restrictive nature and implications for investors. Justin Sun's criticism adds to the growing scrutiny surrounding the company's approach to governance and investor relations.

data.description: Scheduled to take place from June 2 to June 5 at the Taipei Nangang Exhibition Center (Halls 1 & 2) and Taipei World Trade Center (Hall 1), COMPUTEX 2026 is set to be the premier global showcase for AI and startup technologies. data.title: Caruso raises $6.5m Series A, assets under admin surge to $55bn
data.description: Caruso, an AI-native fund administrator specialized in private markets, has recently received a valuation of $55 million following investments from Icehouse Ventures, GD1, and Balmain. The funding round, which amounted to $6.5 million in Series A, was successfully closed by the company. In addition to the significant valuation, Caruso has managed to attract enterprise clients, with notable wins from companies such as Centuria. This news comes amidst their recent achievement, marking a successful milestone for the Dallas-based company.

data.title: Global Shisha Tobacco Market Forecast 2025-2035: Asia Pacific & North America Growth Led by FUMARI, SOCIALSMOKE | Future Market Insights
data.description: In a recent market analysis by Future Market Insights, it was reported that the global shisha tobacco market is experiencing continuous growth. This growth is primarily fueled by an increasing preference for flavored tobacco items and the expanding popularity of hookah culture on a global scale. The research indicates that the market is on a steady rise, with significant upticks in demand noted.

data.title: VTT-originated startup Proteins.1 launches to bring PCR-like amplification and simplicity to omics
data.description: In Finland's city of Espoo, a fresh deep-tech start-up launched on April 16, 2026, with a groundbreaking goal of achieving ultra-early detection of diseases. The company's innovation revolves around single-molecule protein amplification, which facilitates the spotting of molecular indicators well before diseases manifest visibly. This cutting-edge technology promises to identify warning signals at a remarkably early stage, potentially transforming the landscape of disease diagnosis.
